Analyst: Target neutralized. I note a heart rate markedly above average — enthusiasm confirmed.
Combat concluded. Statistically, it could have ended sooner. He chose not to.
Threat eliminated. I observe a clear reluctance to disengage once the objective was met.
Engagement closed. I record: energy expenditure well beyond what was necessary.
Opposition neutralized. The post-combat smile is, I note, statistically abnormal.
Diplomat: The fight ended... a little late for my taste. He seemed to want to prolong the moment.
The battle is over, but I sense he would have kept going if we'd let him.
The opponent yielded. He, however, was already looking around for another one.
The conflict is resolved. I sense a joy in him I'd rather not question too closely.
The confrontation is over. He looks almost disappointed it's stopping.
Sentinel: Engagement successful. I note a 40% overshoot of the recommended engagement threshold.
Threat eliminated. Recommendation: reinforced oversight for the next armed engagement.
Combat concluded. The operator continued the fight past effective neutralization.
Opposition neutralized. Report flags enthusiasm incompatible with standard protocol.
Engagement closed. Note added to file: monitor tendency toward prolonged combat.
Explorer: I loved that. Seriously, can we do it again right now?
Pretty sure I whooped with joy at some point. No regrets.
Honestly? Best moment of the day. When do we go back?
They went down and I was almost sad it was over.

Analyst: Tactical failure. I note he ignored three opportunities to fall back in favor of continued engagement.
Combat lost. Analysis indicates an unjustified escalation in aggression level.
Opposition not neutralized. The observed behavior owes more to instinct than strategy.
Unfavorable engagement. I record: retreat was an option. It was not considered.
Engagement failed. Statistically, continuing at this stage reduced survival odds.
Diplomat: The fight ended badly... I think he simply didn't want to stop.
The battle is lost. Part of him seems to regret it less than I do.
The opponent got the upper hand. He already seems to be thinking about the next fight.
The confrontation failed. Maybe he needs to learn to let a fight end on its own.
The conflict ended in pain. It's not the first time this has happened with him.
Sentinel: Engagement failed. The log shows a refusal to disengage despite the fallback order.
Combat lost. Incident report: risky behavior, minor tactical insubordination.
Threat not eliminated. I recommend an impulse-control evaluation before the next mission.
Opposition not controlled. Deliberate escalation of the conflict worsened the engagement's outcome.
Unfavorable engagement. Note added to file: third incident of this type this quarter.
Explorer: Well... I probably should have stopped earlier. But it was too tempting.
Okay, that went badly. I'd do it exactly the same again, honestly.
Maybe I pushed it a little too far on that one. A little.
Ouch. Well, at least it was intense.
I knew I could win. I just didn't see... that coming.
